thesis: First Majestic Silver is the world's largest primary silver producer, offering the purest leverage to silver price appreciation among large-cap miners. Q1 2026 demonstrated a dramatic operational turnaround with revenue up 95% YoY, net income of $128.1M, and profit margins expanding to 27%. Silver is in a structural supply deficit driven by solar, EV, and AI demand, while AG trades at a discounted valuation ~50% off 52-week highs. The recent Del Toro sale streamlines the portfolio and strengthens the balance sheet.

drivers: Silver breaks out to $45–50/oz on supply deficit realization and investment demand surge
AG's operating leverage drives EPS to $0.80–1.00
Market re-rates AG to 25–30x P/E (historical avg for silver miners in bull markets)
Exploration success at San Dimas or Santa Elena extends mine life and reserves

assumptions: AG delivers 13–15M oz silver annually with AISC of $14–16/oz
EPS of $0.50–0.65 supports 25–35x P/E (sector average for growth miners)
Debt continues to decline; balance sheet strengthens

risks: Silver retreats to $25–28/oz on macro headwinds (strong dollar, recession)
Margins compress but remain positive due to low AISC and gold by-product credits
Mexican regulatory changes increase costs or restrict operations

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings Release — confirmation of production trajectory and margin sustainability
2027 Production Guidance — potential guidance raise if silver prices remain elevated
Silver price movements above $35/oz drive direct margin expansion
Debt reduction initiatives improve balance sheet and credit profile

key_risks: Silver price volatility — a 20% price drop would compress margins significantly
Mexican regulatory risk — mining reforms, tax changes, or environmental restrictions
Operational risk — underground mining accidents, flooding, or grade variability
Foreign exchange risk — Mexican peso appreciation could compress margins
Exploration risk — reserves deplete without replacement; exploration may not yield economic discoveries
